sentences.sentence: The pyramids are the most famous monuments of ancient Egypt and still hold enormous interest for people in the present day.
sentences.translation: 金字塔是古埃及最著名的纪念建筑，直到今天仍然让人们怀有极大的兴趣。

structure.text: The pyramids
are the most famous monuments of ancient Egypt
and still hold enormous interest for people in the present day

difficult_points: are 和 hold 是并列谓语，共同说明金字塔的地位和现代吸引力。
the most famous monuments of ancient Egypt 是最高级结构，强调金字塔在古埃及遗迹中的代表性。
hold enormous interest 表示“令人非常感兴趣”，不是字面上的“拿着兴趣”。

sentences.sentence: These grand, impressive tributes to the memory of the Egyptian kings have become linked with the country even though other cultures, such as the Chinese and Mayan, also built pyramids.
sentences.translation: 这些宏大而令人印象深刻、纪念埃及国王的建筑已经与埃及这个国家紧密相连，尽管中国和玛雅等其他文化也建造过金字塔。

structure.text: These grand, impressive tributes to the memory of the Egyptian kings
have become linked with the country
even though other cultures, such as the Chinese and Mayan, also built pyramids
such as the Chinese and Mayan

difficult_points: These grand, impressive tributes 指代金字塔，并把它们理解为纪念埃及国王的建筑。
have become linked with 使用现在完成时，表示这种联系已经形成并延续到现在。
even though 引导让步状语从句，指出其他文化也有金字塔，但不影响埃及金字塔的代表性。
such as the Chinese and Mayan 是插入的举例说明，列出其他文化。

sentences.sentence: The evolution of the pyramid form has been written and argued about for centuries.
sentences.translation: 几个世纪以来，人们一直书写并争论金字塔形式的演变。

structure.text: The evolution of the pyramid form
has been written and argued about
for centuries

difficult_points: has been written and argued about 是现在完成时的被动结构，表示长期以来一直被讨论。
written and argued about 是并列过去分词，共同作用于同一个主语。
for centuries 强调争论持续了数百年。

sentences.sentence: However, there is no question that, as far as Egypt is concerned, it began with one monument to one king designed by one brilliant architect: the Step Pyramid of Djoser at Saqqara.
sentences.translation: 然而，毫无疑问，就埃及而言，这一切始于一座由一位杰出建筑师为一位国王设计的纪念建筑：萨卡拉的左塞尔阶梯金字塔。

structure.text: However
there is no question
that, as far as Egypt is concerned, it began with one monument to one king designed by one brilliant architect
as far as Egypt is concerned
the Step Pyramid of Djoser at Saqqara

difficult_points: there is no question that 表示“毫无疑问”，that 后说明确定的内容。
as far as Egypt is concerned 是插入语，把讨论范围限定为埃及。
it 指代金字塔形式的演变或埃及金字塔传统。
冒号后的 the Step Pyramid of Djoser at Saqqara 是对 one monument 的具体说明。

sentences.sentence: Djoser was the first king of the Third Dynasty of Egypt and the first to build in stone.
sentences.translation: 左塞尔是埃及第三王朝的第一位国王，也是第一位用石头建造陵墓的国王。

structure.text: Djoser
was the first king of the Third Dynasty of Egypt
and the first to build in stone
to build in stone

difficult_points: and the first to build in stone 省略了 was，与前面的 was the first king 并列。
to build in stone 修饰 the first，说明他在建筑材料上的开创性。
in stone 表示“用石头”，不是地点。

sentences.sentence: Prior to Djoser's reign, tombs were rectangular monuments made of dried clay brick, which covered underground passages where the deceased person was buried.
sentences.translation: 在左塞尔统治之前，陵墓是由晒干的黏土砖制成的矩形纪念建筑，覆盖着埋葬逝者的地下通道。

structure.text: Prior to Djoser's reign
tombs
were rectangular monuments made of dried clay brick
which covered underground passages
where the deceased person was buried

difficult_points: Prior to 是介词短语，表示时间上的“在……之前”。
made of dried clay brick 是过去分词短语，修饰 monuments。
which 引导定语从句，说明这些陵墓覆盖地下通道。
where 从句修饰 underground passages，说明逝者被埋葬的位置。

sentences.sentence: For reasons which remain unclear, Djoser's main official, whose name was Imhotep, conceived of building a taller, more impressive tomb for his king by stacking stone slabs on top of one another, progressively making them smaller, to form the shape now known as the Step Pyramid.
sentences.translation: 出于仍不清楚的原因，左塞尔的主要官员——名叫伊姆霍特普——构想通过把石板一层层堆叠起来并逐渐缩小尺寸，为他的国王建造一座更高、更令人印象深刻的陵墓，从而形成如今被称为阶梯金字塔的形状。

structure.text: For reasons which remain unclear
Djoser's main official
whose name was Imhotep
conceived of building a taller, more impressive tomb for his king
by stacking stone slabs on top of one another
to form the shape now known as the Step Pyramid

difficult_points: which remain unclear 修饰 reasons，说明原因至今不明。
whose name was Imhotep 是非限制性定语从句，补充说明 Djoser's main official 的姓名。
conceived of 后接动名词 building，表示构想某个建造方案。
by stacking ... 和 progressively making ... 说明形成阶梯形状的方法。

sentences.sentence: Djoser is thought to have reigned for 19 years, but some historians and scholars attribute a much longer time for his rule, owing to the number and size of the monuments he built.
sentences.translation: 人们认为左塞尔在位 19 年，但一些历史学家和学者根据他所建纪念建筑的数量和规模，认为他的统治时间要长得多。

structure.text: Djoser
is thought to have reigned for 19 years
but some historians and scholars attribute a much longer time for his rule
owing to the number and size of the monuments he built
he built

difficult_points: is thought to have reigned 是被动表达，表示“被认为曾经统治”。
but 引出学者中的不同观点。
attribute ... owing to ... 表示把更长统治时间的判断归因于建筑的数量和规模。
he built 是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰 monuments。

sentences.sentence: The Step Pyramid has been thoroughly examined and investigated over the last century, and it is now known that the building process went through many different stages.
sentences.translation: 在过去一个世纪里，阶梯金字塔已被彻底考察和研究，现在人们知道其建造过程经历了许多不同阶段。

structure.text: The Step Pyramid
has been thoroughly examined and investigated over the last century
and it is now known
that the building process went through many different stages

difficult_points: has been thoroughly examined and investigated 是现在完成时被动语态，强调研究已经持续并形成结果。
it is now known 是形式主语结构，真正被知道的内容在 that 从句中。
went through many different stages 表示建造过程经历了多个阶段。

sentences.sentence: Historian Marc Van de Mieroop comments on this, writing 'Much experimentation was involved, which is especially clear in the construction of the pyramid in the center of the complex.
sentences.translation: 历史学家 Marc Van de Mieroop 对此评论道：“其中包含了大量试验，这一点在建筑群中心金字塔的建造中尤为明显。

structure.text: Historian Marc Van de Mieroop
comments on this
writing 'Much experimentation was involved
which is especially clear in the construction of the pyramid in the center of the complex

difficult_points: writing 引出 Marc Van de Mieroop 的原话，功能类似补充说明他评论的内容。
Much experimentation was involved 是被动结构，表示建造中包含大量试验。
which 指代前面“大量试验参与其中”这一情况。
in the center of the complex 修饰 pyramid，说明该金字塔所在位置。

sentences.sentence: It had several plans … before it became the first Step Pyramid in history, piling six levels on top of one another …
sentences.translation: 它曾有过几个方案……之后才成为历史上第一座阶梯金字塔，把六层一层层叠加起来……

structure.text: It
had several plans
before it became the first Step Pyramid in history
piling six levels on top of one another

difficult_points: It 指代金字塔或其建造方案。
before 引导时间状语从句，说明最终形态形成之前经历了多个方案。
piling six levels on top of one another 是现在分词短语，说明阶梯金字塔的形成方式。
省略号表示引文中有省略，句意需要结合前后引语理解。

sentences.sentence: The weight of the enormous mass was a challenge for the builders, who placed the stones at an inward incline in order to prevent the monument breaking up.'
sentences.translation: 巨大体量的重量对建造者来说是一个挑战，他们将石块向内倾斜放置，以防止纪念建筑崩裂。”

structure.text: The weight of the enormous mass
was a challenge for the builders
who placed the stones at an inward incline
in order to prevent the monument breaking up

difficult_points: The weight of the enormous mass 是主语，核心词是 weight。
who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 builders 如何应对重量问题。
at an inward incline 表示石块向内倾斜的放置角度。
in order to 引出目的，说明这样放置是为了防止建筑崩裂。

sentences.sentence: When finally completed, the Step Pyramid rose 62 meters high and was the tallest structure of its time.
sentences.translation: 最终完工时，阶梯金字塔高达 62 米，是当时最高的建筑。

structure.text: When finally completed
the Step Pyramid
rose 62 meters high
and was the tallest structure of its time

difficult_points: When finally completed 是省略结构，相当于 when it was finally completed。
rose 62 meters high 表示高度达到 62 米。
of its time 表示“在它所处的时代”，即当时。

sentences.sentence: The complex in which it was built was the size of a city in ancient Egypt and included a temple, courtyards, shrines, and living quarters for the priests.
sentences.translation: 建造它的建筑群有古埃及一座城市那么大，包括一座神庙、庭院、神龛以及祭司的生活区。

structure.text: The complex in which it was built
in which it was built
was the size of a city in ancient Egypt
and included a temple, courtyards, shrines, and living quarters for the priests

difficult_points: in which it was built 修饰 The complex，it 指代阶梯金字塔。
was the size of a city 表示规模相当于一座城市。
included 后列举建筑群内的多个组成部分。
living quarters for the priests 指祭司居住区域。

sentences.sentence: It covered a region of 16 hectares and was surrounded by a wall 10.5 meters high.
sentences.translation: 它占地 16 公顷，周围环绕着一堵 10.5 米高的墙。

structure.text: It
covered a region of 16 hectares
and was surrounded by a wall 10.5 meters high
10.5 meters high

difficult_points: It 指代整个建筑群。
covered a region of 16 hectares 表示占地面积。
was surrounded by 是被动结构，说明建筑群被墙围绕。
10.5 meters high 后置修饰 wall，说明墙的高度。

sentences.sentence: The wall had 13 false doors cut into it with only one true entrance cut into the south-east corner; the entire wall was then ringed by a trench 750 meters long and 40 meters wide.
sentences.translation: 这堵墙上开凿了 13 扇假门，只有一个真正的入口开在东南角；随后整堵墙又被一条长 750 米、宽 40 米的壕沟环绕。

structure.text: The wall
had 13 false doors cut into it
with only one true entrance cut into the south-east corner
the entire wall was then ringed by a trench 750 meters long and 40 meters wide

difficult_points: cut into it 是过去分词短语，说明假门被开凿在墙上。
with only one true entrance ... 补充说明真正入口的数量和位置。
分号连接两个密切相关的独立分句，后一分句说明墙外还有壕沟。
750 meters long and 40 meters wide 后置修饰 trench。

sentences.sentence: The false doors and the trench were incorporated into the complex to discourage unwanted visitors.
sentences.translation: 假门和壕沟被纳入建筑群设计中，用来阻止不受欢迎的访客。

structure.text: The false doors and the trench
were incorporated into the complex
to discourage unwanted visitors

difficult_points: were incorporated into 是被动语态，表示这些设计被纳入建筑群。
to discourage unwanted visitors 是目的状语，说明设计目的。
unwanted visitors 在语境中指试图闯入陵墓的人。

sentences.sentence: If someone wished to enter, he or she would have needed to know in advance how to find the location of the true opening in the wall.
sentences.translation: 如果有人想进入，他或她就必须事先知道如何找到墙上真正入口的位置。

structure.text: If someone wished to enter
he or she
would have needed to know in advance
how to find the location of the true opening in the wall

difficult_points: If 引导条件状语从句，说明进入的前提。
would have needed to 表示在设想情境中“本来需要”。
in advance 表示必须提前知道。
how to find ... 是疑问词加不定式结构，作 know 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: Djoser was so proud of his accomplishment that he broke the tradition of having only his own name on the monument and had Imhotep's name carved on it as well.
sentences.translation: 左塞尔对自己的成就如此自豪，以至于他打破了纪念建筑上只刻自己名字的传统，也让伊姆霍特普的名字刻在其上。

structure.text: Djoser
was so proud of his accomplishment
that he broke the tradition of having only his own name on the monument
and had Imhotep's name carved on it as well

difficult_points: so ... that ... 构成结果结构，表示自豪程度导致后面的行为。
the tradition of having only his own name on the monument 说明被打破的传统内容。
had Imhotep's name carved 是 have something done 结构，表示让别人刻上名字。
as well 表示除了自己的名字之外也刻上了 Imhotep 的名字。

sentences.sentence: The burial chamber of the tomb, where the king's body was laid to rest, was dug beneath the base of the pyramid, surrounded by a vast maze of long tunnels that had rooms off them to discourage robbers.
sentences.translation: 陵墓的墓室位于金字塔底座下方，国王的遗体在那里安葬；墓室周围是一大片由长隧道构成的迷宫，隧道旁还分出房间，用来阻止盗墓者。

structure.text: The burial chamber of the tomb
where the king's body was laid to rest
was dug beneath the base of the pyramid
surrounded by a vast maze of long tunnels
that had rooms off them to discourage robbers

difficult_points: where 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 burial chamber 的用途。
was dug beneath 是被动结构，说明墓室被挖在金字塔底座下。
surrounded by ... 是过去分词短语，描述墓室周围的结构。
that had rooms off them 修饰 tunnels，them 指代 long tunnels。

sentences.sentence: One of the most mysterious discoveries found inside the pyramid was a large number of stone vessels.
sentences.translation: 在金字塔内部发现的最神秘事物之一，是大量石制容器。

structure.text: One of the most mysterious discoveries found inside the pyramid
found inside the pyramid
was a large number of stone vessels

difficult_points: One of ... 作主语时核心是 One，因此谓语用 was。
found inside the pyramid 是过去分词短语，修饰 discoveries。
a large number of stone vessels 是表语，说明神秘发现的具体内容。

sentences.sentence: Over 40,000 of these vessels, of various forms and shapes, were discovered in storerooms off the pyramid's underground passages.
sentences.translation: 这些容器中有 4 万多个形态各异，被发现于金字塔地下通道旁的储藏室中。

structure.text: Over 40,000 of these vessels
of various forms and shapes
were discovered in storerooms
off the pyramid's underground passages

difficult_points: Over 40,000 表示数量超过四万，修饰 these vessels。
of various forms and shapes 是插入性修饰，说明容器形态多样。
were discovered 是被动语态。
off the pyramid's underground passages 表示储藏室从地下通道旁分出。

sentences.sentence: They are inscribed with the names of rulers from the First and Second Dynasties of Egypt and made from different kinds of stone.
sentences.translation: 它们刻有埃及第一、第二王朝统治者的名字，并由不同种类的石头制成。

structure.text: They
are inscribed with the names of rulers from the First and Second Dynasties of Egypt
and made from different kinds of stone

difficult_points: They 指代前文的 stone vessels。
are inscribed with 是被动结构，表示容器上刻有名字。
made from different kinds of stone 省略了 are，与 are inscribed 并列。
from the First and Second Dynasties of Egypt 修饰 rulers，说明统治者所属王朝。

sentences.sentence: There is no agreement among scholars and archaeologists on why the vessels were placed in the tomb of Djoser or what they were supposed to represent.
sentences.translation: 学者和考古学家对于这些容器为何被放在左塞尔的陵墓中，或它们本应代表什么，并没有一致意见。

structure.text: There is no agreement among scholars and archaeologists
on why the vessels were placed in the tomb of Djoser
or what they were supposed to represent

difficult_points: There is no agreement 表示“没有一致意见”。
on 后接 why 和 what 引导的两个并列疑问从句，说明分歧内容。
were placed 是被动语态，强调容器被放置在墓中。
were supposed to represent 表示“本应代表”，带有推测或预设含义。

sentences.sentence: The archaeologist Jean-Philippe Lauer, who excavated most of the pyramid and complex, believes they were originally stored and then given a 'proper burial' by Djoser in his pyramid to honor his predecessors.
sentences.translation: 考古学家 Jean-Philippe Lauer 挖掘了金字塔及其建筑群的大部分区域；他认为这些容器原本被储存起来，后来由左塞尔在自己的金字塔中给予“正式安葬”，以纪念他的前任统治者。

structure.text: The archaeologist Jean-Philippe Lauer
who excavated most of the pyramid and complex
believes
they were originally stored and then given a 'proper burial' by Djoser in his pyramid
to honor his predecessors

difficult_points: who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充 Jean-Philippe Lauer 的考古工作。
believes 后省略 that，引出他的观点。
were originally stored and then given 是并列被动结构，说明容器的两个阶段。
to honor his predecessors 表示这样做的目的。

sentences.sentence: There are other historians, however, who claim the vessels were dumped into the shafts as yet another attempt to prevent grave robbers from getting to the king's burial chamber.
sentences.translation: 然而，也有其他历史学家声称，这些容器被倾倒进竖井中，是又一次防止盗墓者进入国王墓室的尝试。

structure.text: There are other historians
however
who claim the vessels were dumped into the shafts
as yet another attempt to prevent grave robbers from getting to the king's burial chamber

difficult_points: There are other historians 引出另一派观点。
however 是插入语，标记与前一句观点相反或不同。
who claim 后接省略 that 的宾语从句，说明这些历史学家的主张。
to prevent grave robbers from getting to ... 是不定式短语，说明 attempt 的目的。

sentences.sentence: Unfortunately, all of the precautions and intricate design of the underground network did not prevent ancient robbers from finding a way in.
sentences.translation: 遗憾的是，地下网络的所有预防措施和精巧设计都没能阻止古代盗贼找到进入的方法。

structure.text: Unfortunately
all of the precautions and intricate design of the underground network
did not prevent ancient robbers from finding a way in
from finding a way in

difficult_points: Unfortunately 表示作者对结果的评价。
all of the precautions and intricate design 是并列主语，核心是 precautions 和 design。
prevent ... from ... 表示阻止某人做某事。
finding a way in 指找到进入地下网络或陵墓的方法。

sentences.sentence: Djoser's grave goods, and even his body, were stolen at some point in the past and all archaeologists found were a small number of his valuables overlooked by the thieves.
sentences.translation: 左塞尔的陪葬品，甚至他的遗体，都在过去某个时候被盗走了；考古学家发现的只是少量被盗贼忽略的贵重物品。

structure.text: Djoser's grave goods, and even his body
were stolen at some point in the past
and all archaeologists found were a small number of his valuables overlooked by the thieves
overlooked by the thieves

difficult_points: and even his body 是插入式并列，强调被盗的不只是陪葬品。
were stolen 是被动语态，说明物品和遗体被盗。
all archaeologists found 是主语从句式表达，意思是“考古学家所找到的一切”。
overlooked by the thieves 修饰 valuables，表示这些贵重物品被盗贼漏掉。

sentences.sentence: There was enough left throughout the pyramid and its complex, however, to astonish and amaze the archaeologists who excavated it.
sentences.translation: 然而，金字塔及其建筑群各处仍留下了足够多的东西，令发掘它的考古学家感到惊讶和赞叹。

structure.text: There was enough left throughout the pyramid and its complex
however
to astonish and amaze the archaeologists
who excavated it

difficult_points: There was enough left 表示仍有足够多的遗留物。
however 插入句中，形成与前一句被盗严重的转折。
to astonish and amaze 表示结果或程度：足以让考古学家震惊。
who excavated it 修饰 archaeologists，it 指代金字塔及其建筑群。

sentences.sentence: Egyptologist Miroslav Verner writes, 'Few monuments hold a place in human history as significant as that of the Step Pyramid in Saqqara …
sentences.translation: 埃及学家 Miroslav Verner 写道：“很少有纪念建筑在人类历史中占据像萨卡拉阶梯金字塔那样重要的地位……

structure.text: Egyptologist Miroslav Verner
writes
Few monuments hold a place in human history
as significant as that of the Step Pyramid in Saqqara

difficult_points: writes 引出直接引语，后面的内容是 Verner 的评价。
Few monuments 是否定意义的数量表达，表示“很少有纪念建筑”。
as significant as ... 是同级比较，强调阶梯金字塔的重要性。
that 指代 a place in human history，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: It can be said without exaggeration that this pyramid complex constitutes a milestone in the evolution of monumental stone architecture in Egypt and in the world as a whole.'
sentences.translation: 可以毫不夸张地说，这个金字塔建筑群构成了埃及乃至整个世界巨型石质建筑演变中的一个里程碑。”

structure.text: It can be said without exaggeration
that this pyramid complex constitutes a milestone in the evolution of monumental stone architecture in Egypt and in the world as a whole
in Egypt and in the world as a whole

difficult_points: It can be said 是形式主语加被动结构，真正内容在 that 从句中。
without exaggeration 表示“毫不夸张地”。
constitutes a milestone 表示构成一个里程碑，强调历史意义。
as a whole 修饰 the world，表示从整体来看。

sentences.sentence: The Step Pyramid was a revolutionary advance in architecture and became the archetype which all the other great pyramid builders of Egypt would follow.
sentences.translation: 阶梯金字塔是建筑上的一次革命性进步，并成为埃及其他伟大金字塔建造者都会效仿的原型。

structure.text: The Step Pyramid
was a revolutionary advance in architecture
and became the archetype
which all the other great pyramid builders of Egypt would follow

difficult_points: was 和 became 是并列谓语，说明阶梯金字塔既是进步也是范式。
revolutionary advance 表示具有根本改变意义的进步。
which 引导定语从句修饰 archetype，说明后来的建造者会遵循这一原型。
would follow 表示后来者将会效仿。
